title: Security Misconfiguration

explain.what: The server is set up in a way that leaks internal information or allows unauthorized access. Common causes: default credentials left unchanged, verbose error pages, unnecessary admin endpoints exposed.
explain.example: Triggering any error causes the server to return the full stack trace, the server's file paths, and all environment variables — including database passwords and API keys.
explain.impact: Information disclosure · unauthorized admin access · credential theft from leaked env vars

vulnerable.annotation: ⚠ The catch block returns the full error stack and process.env to the client.

fixed.annotation: ✅ Log the error server-side only. Return a generic message to the client.

demoHint: Click 'Trigger Error' to see what the vulnerable server leaks. Then compare with the fixed version.
